reason: Response B reflects Claude Haiku's typical pattern of giving a clear, factual, educational answer to an open-ended prompt — straightforward and informative without being unnecessarily philosophical. Response A reads more like a stylized, literary model trying to seem clever and introspective, which feels more like an imitation of a "small but witty" model rather than Haiku's genuine tendency toward direct, helpful factual responses. Haiku tends to answer questions at face value rather than meta-commenting on the nature of truth.
